eb/myAccessor/MyAccessor.js@@ to change the functionality #Double Click on the MyAccessor Icon and hit Reload to get the new functionality ! [[#Modules]] Modules Some Accessors require JavaScript modules, which, in Ptolemy, are found in @@$PTII/ptole

les, which, in Ptolemy, are found in @@$PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules@@. For example, @@$PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/net/Discovery.js@@ contains: (:source lang="javascript":) var discovery = require('discovery'); (:sourceend:) If

discovery = require('discovery'); (:sourceend:) If you did the above steps and created @@$PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/myDiscovery/MyDiscovery.js@@, you could replace (:source lang="javascript":) var discovery = require('discovery'); (:

script":) var discovery = require('discovery'); (:sourceend:) with (:source lang="javascript":) var discovery = require('myDiscovery'); (:sourceend:) and then create a copy of @@$PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/discovery@@: (:source lang="bash":)

b/jjs/modules/discovery@@: (:source lang="bash":) cd $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules mkdir myDiscovery cp discovery/discovery.js myDiscovery/myDiscovery.js cp discovery/package.json myDiscovery/package.json (:sourceend:) Then edit both files in m

ckage.json (:sourceend:) Then edit both files in myDiscovery/ and change @@discovery@@ to @@myDiscovery@@. Restore September 08, 2015, at 10:48 pm by Zoran Krivokapic - Changed lines 64-65 from: ! [[#ImportTheNewAccessor]] Import the new Accessor to:

y II has a number of different steps !Install Ptolemy II See [[Ptolemy II]] for how to install Ptolemy II from the svn devel tree. Ptolemy II 10.0 '''is no up to date''', use the svn devel tree instead. !The TerraSwarm Accessors repository The acces

ad. !The TerraSwarm Accessors repository The accessors are online at http://www.terraswarm.org/accessors. If Ptolemy II is built from the Ptolemy II svn repository, then a copy of http://www.terraswarm.org/accessors is checked out in @@$PTII/org/terr

m.org/accessors is checked out in @@$PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web@@ and when you reload an accessor, the local copy is used. The reason this is done is so as to make it easier to develop accessors that will be eventually added to the h

p accessors that will be eventually added to the http://www.terraswarm.org/accessors website. If you check out the ptII svn repository via anonymous, read-only access, then the copy of http://www.terraswarm.org/accessors will be checked out anonymous

aswarm.org/accessors will be checked out anonymously. If you check out the ptII svn repository via read/write access, then the copy of the http://www.terraswarm.org/accessors will be checked out read-write if you have read/write access to that repo.

write if you have read/write access to that repo. The copy of http://www.terraswarm.org/accessors is checked out when @@./configure@@ is run, when a model that contains an accessor is loaded or when an accessor is reloaded. So, to get started, either

n accessor is reloaded. So, to get started, either run: (:source lang="bash":) cd $PTII ./configure (:sourceend:) Or open a model that has accessors, such as (:source lang="bash":) $PTII/bin/vergil org/terraswarm/accessor/demo/StockTick/StockTick.xml

g/terraswarm/accessor/demo/StockTick/StockTick.xml (:sourceend:) This will create or update @@$PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web@@: (:source lang="bash":) bash-3.2$ ls $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web Accessor_1.dtd camer
cessor/accessors/web Accessor_1.dtd cameras jsdoc svn.html README.md copyright.txt localization updateIndex audio demo net
demo net utilities basicFunctions.js devices obsolete vendors basicFunctionsPtDoc.xml doc renderHTML.xsl wiki browserHost.js i
erHTML.xsl wiki browserHost.js image robotics browserHostPtDoc.xml index.html services build.xml index.json signals bash-3.2$ (:sourceend:) ''The precise contents of the d

3.2$ (:sourceend:) ''The precise contents of the directory may change, but the key thing to note is that there is an @@index.json@@ file and there are subdirectories.'' ! Create a copy of an accessor One way to learn about accessors is to copy someth

One way to learn about accessors is to copy something that works and use it. In this example, we will create a copy of the StockTick accessor # Go to @@$PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web@@ (:source lang="bash") cd $PTII/org/terraswarm/acces

:source lang="bash") cd $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web (:sourceend:) ''Hint: If this directory does not exist, then be sure that you have checked out the ptII svn repo and not installed from one of the installers. Also, try running @@cd

rom one of the installers. Also, try running @@cd $PTII;./configure@@ and try opening $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/demo/StockTick/StockTick.xml'' # Create the test directory: (:source lang="bash":) mkdir myAccessor (:source:) # Copy these files: (:

mkdir myAccessor (:source:) # Copy these files: (:source lang="bash":) cp services/StockTick.js myAccessor/MyAccessor.js cp services/StockTickIcon.xml myAccessor/MyAccessorIcon.xml cp services/index.json myAccessor/ (:sourceend:) ## services/StockTic

son myAccessor/ (:sourceend:) ## services/StockTick.js defines the accessor, we create a copy. Note that the directory name (@@myAccessor@@) starts with a lower case letter, but the .js file (@@MyAccessor.js@@) starts with an upper case letter. This

ssor.js@@) starts with an upper case letter. This is the coding standard. ## services/StockTickIcon.xml defines an icon for the accessor. ## services/index.json lists the accessors in the directory that are available for import. # Edit @@myAccessor/

hat are available for import. # Edit @@myAccessor/MyAccessor.js@@ and change @@StockTick@@ to @@MyAccessor@@. # Edit @@myAccessor/index.json so that it refers to @@MyAccessor.js@@ (:source lang="xml":) [ "MyAccessor.js", ] (:sourceend:) # Start up Pt
